title: Sweet Velvety Australian Lamb Tagine
description: A rich and spicy lamb tagine infused with the sweetness of Australian honey and the warmth of cinnamon. Perfect for a romantic dinner.

ingredients.name: Ground lamb
ingredients.notes: You can also use beef or mutton for a different flavor.

ingredients.name: Ghee or ground nut oil
ingredients.notes: Ground nut oil adds a nutty flavor and aroma to the dish.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: You can use 1 large onion if you prefer.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: You can use 2 cloves if you prefer a milder flavor.

ingredients.name: Honey
ingredients.notes: Australian honey has a rich, caramel flavor that pairs perfectly with lamb.

ingredients.name: Ground cinnamon
ingredients.notes: Cinnamon is a classic spice in Australian cooking.

ingredients.name: Ground ginger
ingredients.notes: Ginger adds a warm, spicy flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: You can use less salt if you prefer.

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: You can use more pepper if you prefer.

ingredients.name: Plain yogurt
ingredients.notes: You can use Greek yogurt for a thicker consistency.

ingredients.name: Fresh cilantro
ingredients.notes: You can use parsley or dill for a different flavor.

instructions: Heat the ground nut oil in a large clay or ceramic pot over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until it's softened and translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the lamb and cook until it's browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the honey, cinnamon, ginger, salt, and pepper. Stir well to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the yogurt and cook for another 5 minutes, until heated through.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve over couscous or rice.
